summary: Matched Peach's parasol item by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itpeachparasol.s with src/melee/it/items/itpeachparasol.c and a new item-specific header. The PR moved the object file from asm to C in obj_files.mk, exposed the parasol item state table and functions through itpeachparasol.h, and cleaned up ad-hoc forward declarations in ftcommon.c and it_803F3100.c. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 339-line handwritten assembly file for itpeachparasol and added a 147-line C implementation.
Changed obj_files.mk so itpeachparasol builds from src/melee/it/items/itpeachparasol.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itpeachparasol.s.o.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F74F8[] in C with three states, all using it_802BDCC0 as the callback and NULL for the other callbacks.
Reimplemented it_802BDA40 as a small lookup through a static int array { 15, 16 } indexed by it_803F74F8[i].msid.

Reimplemented it_802BDA64 as the Peach parasol SpawnItem creation routine, setting kind 0x67, prev_pos/pos, facing_dir, parent gobj fields, zero velocity, damage, flags, then calling Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, and db_80225DD8.

Reimplemented parasol lifecycle and transition helpers: owner cleanup via ftPe_8011D518, item removal via Item_8026A8EC, wrappers for it_8026B724/it_8026B73C/it_8026B894, animation-frame predicate it_802BDC38, and state/animation setup helpers it_802BDD40 and it_802BDDB4.
Added src/melee/it/items/itpeachparasol.h with prototypes and extern ItemStateTable it_803F74F8[].
Included the new parasol header from ftcommon.c, ftPe_SpecialHi.c, and it_803F3100.c, replacing local declarations for parasol symbols.
Removed the old it_802BDA64 prototype from src/melee/it/it_27CF.h, suggesting ownership of this item API moved to the item-specific header.
smart_moves: Kept the asm symbol names for all newly matched functions, preserving linkage and minimizing callsite churn while still moving implementation to C.
Localized Peach parasol declarations into itpeachparasol.h instead of keeping scattered local externs in ftcommon.c and it_803F3100.c.
Represented the former .data ItemStateTable it_803F74F8[] directly as a typed C array, making the state table searchable and reusable by item registry code.
Used forward headers in itpeachparasol.h rather than pulling in heavy full definitions, keeping cross-system item/fighter includes lighter.
Converted the former sdata lookup it_804D5518 into a static local C array inside it_802BDA40, matching its limited use scope.
Preserved small wrapper functions around common item helpers, which likely helps matching by keeping call boundaries and symbol addresses intact.
decomp_lessons: For item files, once the asm is matched, update obj_files.mk from the asm object path to the src C object path in the same PR.
Move item-specific prototypes out of broad aggregate headers when a dedicated item header exists; consumers should include the specific item header.
State tables from assembly can often be represented as ItemStateTable arrays with callback symbols and NULL entries, improving readability without changing external symbol names.

SpawnItem initialization order and field choices matter for matching: this C uses prev_pos copy, pos copy, zeroed velocity components, parent duplication, and flag bit assignment to mirror the assembly.
Do not over-name behavior when evidence is still address-based; this PR retained address-style names such as it_802BDA64 and it_802BDCC0 rather than inventing semantic names.
Fighter code that depends on item functions can include the item-specific header, as shown by ftcommon.c and ftPe_SpecialHi.c including itpeachparasol.h.
naming_conventions: Matched functions retained address-style names: it_802BDA40, it_802BDA64, it_802BDB4C, it_802BDCC0, it_802BDD40, it_802BDDB4, it_802BDDF8.
Global data retained address-style name: it_803F74F8.
Local variable names in the new C are descriptive but conservative: parent, pos, arg2, dir, spawn, item_gobj, item.
The file uses project item typedefs such as Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Vec3, ItemStateTable, SpawnItem, and Item.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a full assembly file with C while preserving all exported labels as C symbols.
Encoded former .sdata constants directly as C literals: 0.0F, 16.0F, and 1.0F.
Used a static local array in it_802BDA40 to mirror the old small data table it_804D5518.
Used chained zero assignment for spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F, matching the repeated zero stores from assembly while remaining compact.
Preserved one-call wrapper functions for it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, and it_8026B894 rather than inlining behavior into callers.
Mapped assembly callback pointer storage at item->on_accessory to a no-op C function it_802BDD3C.
Translated branchy boolean assembly into explicit bool-returning C in it_802BDC38 and it_802BDCC0.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Peach parasol address-style functions to semantic names.
Compare this SpawnItem initialization pattern with other matched item spawn functions.
Search for other PRs moving item-specific declarations out of it_27CF.h into src/melee/it/items/*.h.
Inspect later changes to it_803F74F8 and Peach parasol ItemStateTable callbacks.
Find PRs matching itpeachtoad, itpeachtoadspore, or itpeachturnip for similar Peach item conventions.
